Ecological Multifunctinality Analysis

Marius Munschek

2025-05-30

Preface

This R Script is intended to be used to calculate the ecological multifunctionality for (mostly) soil functions in the Jena experiment. I will focus on the following functions: - Biomass production above and below - Nitrogen leaching - Carbon sequestration -

Preparation

Loading packages

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(brms)
library(performance)
library(ggpmisc)

Set up a theme

theme_set(
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 13, hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 13),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 13),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
    )
)

Load datasets and filter for relevant observations/measurements

# load synthesis dataset
ecologicalMF <- read_xlsx("C:/Users/mm68tulo/Desktop/Jena Chapter/Data Analysis/1_Data/ecological_multifunctionality.xlsx")
economicMF <- read_xlsx("C:/Users/mm68tulo/Desktop/Jena Chapter/Data Analysis/1_Data/economic_MF.xlsx")

Multidiversity Index

Here I want to integrate the Diversity metrics

Statistical Models

Also single functions or just multifunctionality?

fitMFsown <- brm(
  multifunctionality ~ log2(sowndiv),
 ecologicalMF)
## 
## SAMPLING FOR MODEL 'anon_model' NOW (CHAIN 1).
## Chain 1: 
## Chain 1: Gradient evaluation took 3e-05 seconds
## Chain 1: 1000 transitions using 10 leapfrog steps per transition would take 0.3 seconds.
## Chain 1: Adjust your expectations accordingly!
## Chain 1: 
## Chain 1: 
## Chain 1: Iteration:    1 / 2000 [  0%]  (Warmup)
## Chain 1: Iteration:  200 / 2000 [ 10%]  (Warmup)
## Chain 1: Iteration:  400 / 2000 [ 20%]  (Warmup)
## Chain 1: Iteration:  600 / 2000 [ 30%]  (Warmup)
## Chain 1: Iteration:  800 / 2000 [ 40%]  (Warmup)
## Chain 1: Iteration: 1000 / 2000 [ 50%]  (Warmup)
## Chain 1: Iteration: 1001 / 2000 [ 50%]  (Sampling)
## Chain 1: Iteration: 1200 / 2000 [ 60%]  (Sampling)
## Chain 1: Iteration: 1400 / 2000 [ 70%]  (Sampling)
## Chain 1: Iteration: 1600 / 2000 [ 80%]  (Sampling)
## Chain 1: Iteration: 1800 / 2000 [ 90%]  (Sampling)
## Chain 1: Iteration: 2000 / 2000 [100%]  (Sampling)
## Chain 1: 
## Chain 1:  Elapsed Time: 0.027 seconds (Warm-up)
## Chain 1:                0.036 seconds (Sampling)
## Chain 1:                0.063 seconds (Total)
## Chain 1:

## Chain 4: Adjust your expectations accordingly!

## Chain 4: Iteration:    1 / 2000 [  0%]  (Warmup)
## Chain 4: Iteration:  200 / 2000 [ 10%]  (Warmup)
## Chain 4: Iteration:  400 / 2000 [ 20%]  (Warmup)
## Chain 4: Iteration:  600 / 2000 [ 30%]  (Warmup)

## Chain 4: Iteration: 1000 / 2000 [ 50%]  (Warmup)

## Chain 4:
#check model assumptions
    pp_check(fitMFsown, type="scatter_avg") 

    pp_check(fitMFsown, ndraws=50)

    check_model(fitMFsown, check="linearity") 

    check_model(fitMFsown, check="homogeneity")

    plot(fitMFsown)

# check model output
summary(fitMFsown)
##  Family: gaussian 
##   Links: mu = identity; sigma = identity 
## Formula: multifunctionality ~ log2(sowndiv) 
##    Data: ecologicalMF (Number of observations: 78) 
##   Draws: 4 chains, each with iter = 2000; warmup = 1000; thin = 1;
##          total post-warmup draws = 4000
## 
## Regression Coefficients:
##             Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
## Intercept       0.51      0.01     0.48     0.53 1.00     4154     2480
## log2sowndiv     0.04      0.01     0.03     0.05 1.00     4634     2887
## 
## Further Distributional Parameters:
##       Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
## sigma     0.07      0.01     0.06     0.08 1.00     2872     2427
## 
## Draws were sampled using sampling(NUTS). For each parameter, Bulk_ESS
## and Tail_ESS are effective sample size measures, and Rhat is the potential
## scale reduction factor on split chains (at convergence, Rhat = 1).
fitMFreal <- brm(
  multifunctionality ~ log2(meanSR),
  ecologicalMF)
## 
## SAMPLING FOR MODEL 'anon_model' NOW (CHAIN 1).
## Chain 1: 
## Chain 1: Gradient evaluation took 2.9e-05 seconds
## Chain 1: 1000 transitions using 10 leapfrog steps per transition would take 0.29 seconds.

# check model assumptions
    pp_check(fitMFreal, type="scatter_avg") 

    pp_check(fitMFreal, ndraws=50)

    check_model(fitMFreal, check="linearity") 

    check_model(fitMFreal, check="homogeneity")

    plot(fitMFreal)

#check model output
summary(fitMFreal)
##  Family: gaussian 
##   Links: mu = identity; sigma = identity 
## Formula: multifunctionality ~ log2(meanSR) 
##    Data: ecologicalMF (Number of observations: 78) 
##   Draws: 4 chains, each with iter = 2000; warmup = 1000; thin = 1;
##          total post-warmup draws = 4000
## 
## Regression Coefficients:
##            Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
## Intercept      0.51      0.01     0.48     0.53 1.00     4728     2623
## log2meanSR     0.05      0.01     0.04     0.06 1.00     4760     3037
## 
## Further Distributional Parameters:
##       Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
## sigma     0.07      0.01     0.06     0.08 1.00     3596     2842
## 
## Draws were sampled using sampling(NUTS). For each parameter, Bulk_ESS
## and Tail_ESS are effective sample size measures, and Rhat is the potential
## scale reduction factor on split chains (at convergence, Rhat = 1).
# merge ecological and economic multifunctionality data
joinedMF <- ecologicalMF %>%
  left_join(economicMF, by = "plotcode") 

# Correlation between economic and ecological multifunctionality
cor.test(joinedMF$multifunctionality, joinedMF$costs_total_mid)
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  joinedMF$multifunctionality and joinedMF$costs_total_mid
## t = 8.5426, df = 76, p-value = 1.005e-12
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.5654263 0.7981187
## sample estimates:
##       cor 
## 0.6998932

Plotting

Plot biomass against realized species richness

#plot multifunctionality as a response to sowndiv
ggplot(ecologicalMF, aes(x=log2(sowndiv), y=multifunctionality)) +
  geom_point(
    color = "#28a87d",
    alpha = .5,
    shape = "X",
    stroke = 1,
    size = 4
  ) +
   geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    se = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(title = "Ecological Multifunctionality vs. Sown Plant Diversity",
       x = "Log2(Sown Plant Diversity)",
       y = "Ecological Multifunctionality")

# plot MF as a function of meanSR
ggplot(ecologicalMF, aes(x=log2(meanSR), y=multifunctionality)) +
    geom_point(
    color = "#28a87d",
    alpha = .5,
    shape = "X",
    stroke = 1,
    size = 4
  ) +
   geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    se = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(title = "Multifunctionality vs. Realized Species Richness",
       x = "Log2(Realized Species Richness)",
       y = "Multifunctionality")
